results.core_alignment_check: Both address processor-side restrictions on who may access/process personal data, but the regulation specifically requires that any natural person acting under the processor's authority with access to personal data processes them only on the controller's instructions; the contract instead limits access to employees with a need-to-know for performing the processor's obligations, without requiring controller instructions.
results.condition_check: The regulation applies whenever a natural person acting under the processor's authority has access to personal data; the contract applies only to employees for whom access is necessary and relevant to perform contractual obligations. These conditions are not the same.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall ensure' obligation, which is not weaker in force.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds a need-to-know limitation and ties access to performance of obligations under the agreements, but it omits the specific instruction-from-controller constraint required by the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall ensure that access to the Personal Data is restricted to only the employees to whom it is necessary and relevant to process the Personal Data in order for the Data Processor to perform its obligations under the Main Agreement and this Data Processor Agreement.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the parent clause introduces an in-line list of the categories of data subjects, and the sub-item 'End-users' is one such category of persons.
results.condition_check: No condition in the requirement; the clause is unconditional.
results.modality_check: Mandatory by parent clause context ('processes ... about the following categories').
results.other_constraints_check: Matches the required content axis (category of data subjects) and is in-line content, not a pointer elsewhere.
results.contract_source_text: End-users

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the breach notification to include, among other items, the consequences of the breach. The contract sub-item states a description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach, which is the same substantive content item.
results.condition_check: No special triggering condition beyond inclusion in the breach notification/register context; the parent clause requires the register to include the listed items at a minimum, which is compatible.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory ('shall have and maintain' and 'shall at a minimum include'), so the sub-item is mandatory content, not mere permission.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is at least as specific as the regulation on the consequences item; it covers likely and actual consequences, which meets or exceeds the required consequences of the breach.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in prior consultation with supervisory authorities before high-risk processing, while the contract requires assistance with responding to data subject rights requests. Different underlying obligation and recipient/task.
results.condition_check: Different triggering situation: high-risk processing and prior consultation with supervisory authorities versus data subject rights requests.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory assistance obligation, but it is for a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and scope differ; the contract concerns reasonable time for assistance on data subject requests, not consultation prior to processing.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires processing personal data only on documented instructions from the controller. The contract instead says personal data is only processed by the processor at its address and not transferred to third countries/international organizations; it does not require controller instructions or documented instructions.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / no matching instruction condition present.
results.modality_check: The contract uses restrictive language ('only processed', 'does not transfer'), but this is about location and transfer limits, not instruction-based authorization.
results.other_constraints_check: The location and transfer constraints are unrelated to the regulation's instruction-based processing requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Personal Data is only processed by the Data Processor at the Data Processor’s address. The Data Processor does not transfer the Personal Data to third countries or international organizations.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires that the breach notification include several items, one of which is the consequences of the breach. The contract sub-item, read with the parent clause introducing the register, states a description of the likely and actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach. This matches the required consequences content, though only one of the required notification elements is present.
results.condition_check: No special triggering condition beyond inclusion in the breach notification/register; the contract item is unconditional and compatible.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory ('shall have and maintain' / 'shall at a minimum include'), so the sub-item is mandatory content within that register. This is at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall include'.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract provides the required content about consequences; it does not add a conflicting timing, location, or manner constraint. However, it does not cover the other required notification elements, so it only partially overlaps.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract is about providing a register/copy of breach records on request, not assisting the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject
results.condition_check: Different trigger: request for a copy in writing by the controller or data protection agency, versus assistance with communicating a breach to the data subject
results.modality_check: The contract imposes a mandatory disclosure duty, but it is a different duty from the required assistance obligation
results.other_constraints_check: Different object/recipient and purpose; no obligation to help communicate to data subjects
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires processor assistance specifically for notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority, while the contract clause concerns assisting the controller in responding to data subject rights requests by providing information and documentation.
results.condition_check: Different triggering situation: data subject rights requests versus personal data breach notification.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory assistance duties, but modality is irrelevant because the underlying obligation differs.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds reasonable time and information/documentation support, but these do not match the breach-notification context.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
results.core_alignment_check: Same general security/confidentiality obligation: the regulation requires the processor to ensure only authorized persons process personal data and that they are bound by confidentiality; the contract restricts access to personal data to only necessary/relevant employees, which is a related access-control measure but does not expressly require confidentiality commitments or statutory confidentiality obligations.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern persons who are authorized/allowed to process personal data.
results.modality_check: Equal-or-greater force: the contract imposes a mandatory restriction/ensure obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is narrower on target and manner: it limits access to necessary and relevant employees, but omits the specific confidentiality commitment/statutory obligation requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall ensure that access to the Personal Data is restricted to only the employees to whom it is necessary and relevant to process the Personal Data in order for the Data Processor to perform its obligations under the Main Agreement and this Data Processor Agreement.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject, but the contract only requires maintaining a register of breaches and says nothing about assisting communication to data subjects.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition overlap; the contract clause is unconditional and the regulation duty is triggered by a personal data breach communication need.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory 'shall', but it is directed to a different obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing, manner, target, or location constraints for the communication-assistance duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of all Personal Data Breaches. The register shall at a minimum include the following:

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires prior specific or general written authorization from the controller before engaging a sub-processor; the contract instead requires a written sub-processor agreement and monitoring/control of sub-processors, but does not require controller authorization.
results.condition_check: Regulation is triggered before engaging a sub-processor; the contract has no equivalent authorization condition.
results.modality_check: The contract imposes mandatory duties, but they are different duties from the authorization requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds sub-processor agreement, compliance monitoring, and documentation on request, which are related but not the same as prior written authorization.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller. The contract text instead identifies categories/types of personal data in a sub-appendix, limits processing to what is necessary and relevant for the main services, and requires updating the appendix when changes occur. It does not impose or describe documented controller instructions for processing.
results.condition_check: No matching condition: the regulation is conditioned on documented instructions from the controller; the contract is conditioned on necessity/relevance to the main services and appendix updates.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language for its own listed limits, but it does not contain the required instruction-based obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract’s subject matter is different: it is about listing data categories/types and limiting processing scope, not about the source/form of processing instructions or documentation thereof.
results.contract_source_text: (the ”Personal Data”). The categories and types of Personal Data processed by the Data Processor on behalf of the Data Controller are listed in sub-appendix A. The Data Processor only performs processing activities that are necessary and relevant to perform the Main Services. The parties shall update sub-appendix A whenever changes occur that necessitates an update.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: processor/controller relationship and personal data processing are addressed, but the clause only lists categories/types of personal data and limits processing to necessary/relevant main services; it does not state that processing occurs only on documented instructions from the controller
results.condition_check: No matching condition: the regulation requires documented controller instructions as the trigger for processing, while the contract uses necessity/relevance to main services and an appendix update duty
results.modality_check: The contract imposes obligations, but not the specific obligation to process only on documented instructions
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's scope limitation to necessary and relevant processing is not equivalent to the documented-instructions constraint
results.contract_source_text: (the ”Personal Data”). The categories and types of Personal Data processed by the Data Processor on behalf of the Data Controller are listed in sub-appendix A. The Data Processor only performs processing activities that are necessary and relevant to perform the Main Services. The parties shall update sub-appendix A whenever changes occur that necessitates an update.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Different obligation: the regulation requires processing personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, while the contract clause requires the processor to notify without undue delay if it thinks an instruction conflicts with applicable law.
results.condition_check: Regulation has no conflict-with-law trigger; contract is triggered by the processor's belief that an instruction conflicts with applicable law.
results.modality_check: The contract imposes a mandatory notice duty, but it is not the same duty as the regulation's mandatory processing-on-instructions obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing differs but is not the main issue; the substantive object and recipient/action are different.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give notice without undue delay if the Data Processor considers the at the time being Instruction to be in conflict with the Applicable Law. 5. The data Processor’s obligations

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject. The contract requires processor assistance to the controller only when the controller receives a data subject rights request and needs help providing information/documentation for the reply. That is a different assistance duty (data subject rights requests generally), not breach communication to the data subject.
results.condition_check: The triggering condition is different: a data subject rights request versus communication of a personal data breach.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall assist', which is not weaker than the regulation's 'shall assist'.
results.other_constraints_check: The subject matter, recipient, and purpose differ; the contract concerns providing information and documentation for replies to rights requests, not communicating a breach to the data subject.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to take all measures needed to ensure processing security; the contract requires the processor to provide documentation of its security measures on written request. This is related to security, but it is an information/disclosure duty, not the substantive duty to implement security measures.
results.condition_check: Regulation is unconditional; contract only applies if the controller requests documentation in writing, so the triggering condition is different and narrower.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall' for the documentation duty, but that duty is not the same as the required security-measures obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract addresses documentation and disclosure, not the manner/timing of taking security measures themselves.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: processor assistance for handling data subject rights requests: regulation requires assisting the controller in responding; contract requires immediate forwarding of the request to the controller and not responding directly, which is a form of assistance but does not itself cover broader help in fulfilling the response obligation
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by a data subject request to exercise data subject rights.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'must' is mandatory and at least as strong as 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: immediate forwarding is not weaker than the required assistance in this context, though the contract is narrower in scope.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both concern the content of a personal data breach notification/register entry, and the sub-item provides one required element—the nature of the breach—within the parent clause's mandatory register contents.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation requires the notification to include the nature of the breach; the contract requires a description of that nature, with optional additional details if possible.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('shall have and maintain' / 'shall at a minimum include'), so the sub-item is part of a mandatory requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's description of the breach nature is the same content axis as the regulation's required 'nature of personal data breach'; the extra categories/number details do not weaken it.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

shared_groups: Compliance Monitoring Audit And Demonstration
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract requires documentation of compliance to be provided, but it does not state that the processor must make available to the controller the information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition in the regulation beyond the general compliance-demonstration context; the contract's 'within reasonable time' is not the same issue.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', which is mandatory, but it is directed to providing documentation rather than making available necessary information to the controller.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing is compatible or stricter ('within reasonable time'), but the object/recipient mismatch remains.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided within reasonable time.

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires processor assistance to the controller specifically for compliance with DPIA obligations. The contract clause requires processor assistance to the controller, but only in responding to data subject rights requests, not DPIA-related compliance.
results.condition_check: Different triggering situation: data subject rights requests versus DPIA compliance.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall assist' obligation, which is not weaker in force.
results.other_constraints_check: The assistance content (providing necessary information and documentation) and reasonable time are compatible, but they relate to a different task.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract text only gives a heading/topic ('Personal Data Breaches') and does not state any obligation to include the required notification contents.
results.condition_check: No condition is stated in the contract text.
results.modality_check: No modality is stated in the contract text.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, or content constraints are stated in the contract text.
results.contract_source_text: Personal Data Breaches

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both concern the processor supporting the controller on security of processing; providing documentation of security measures is a form of assistance related to ensuring security.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract triggers on a written request, which is a narrower condition than the regulation’s general assistance duty and does not weaken it.
results.modality_check: Equal-or-greater force: 'shall provide' is mandatory.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract specifies one means of assistance (documentation on request) within the broader security-assistance obligation; it does not contradict or loosen the duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller before processing when a legal requirement exists and disclosure is not prohibited on public-interest grounds. The contract instead requires the processor to notify if it considers an instruction conflicts with applicable law; this is a different trigger and subject matter.
results.condition_check: Different condition: legal requirement before processing vs processor's view that an instruction conflicts with applicable law.
results.modality_check: Mandatory notice obligation, but on a different issue.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing differs and is weaker/irrelevant to the specific pre-processing notice requirement; 'without undue delay' is not the same as 'before processing'.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give notice without undue delay if the Data Processor considers the at the time being Instruction to be in conflict with the Applicable Law. 5. The data Processor’s obligations

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to contractually impose Article 28(3)-equivalent obligations on engaged sub-processors; the contract clause only says the processor is accountable to the controller for sub-processors' actions/omissions, which is a liability/accountability rule, not an obligation to flow down contractual terms.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition in the regulation beyond engaging sub-processors; the clause does not address that flow-down condition.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory duty to impose obligations; the clause imposes accountability, not the same mandatory flow-down duty.
results.other_constraints_check: The required manner is by contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law; the clause contains no such contractual/legal-act flow-down requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract sub-item states a description of the likely and actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply to the personal data breach notification/register content without additional limiting conditions.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('shall have and maintain' / 'shall at a minimum include'), so the sub-item content is required.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is at least as specific as the regulation on the consequences element, and the broader parent register context does not weaken that content requirement.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller. The contract instead says the data controller guarantees its own processing complies with law; it does not impose the processor's obligation to act only on documented controller instructions.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / no matching instruction condition is stated.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a guarantee about lawful processing, not an obligation on the processor to follow documented instructions.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent timing, manner, or recipient constraints for the processor-instruction duty are present.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Controller guarantees that the Personal Data transferred to the Data Processor is processed by the Data Controller in accordance with the Applicable Law, including the legislative requirements re lawfulness of processing.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to impose the same Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract or other legal act. The contract requires the Data Processor to conclude a written sub-processor agreement and says that agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as those applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under the DPA. This is the same real-world obligation and is at least as strict.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both apply when engaging sub-processors; the contract covers any sub-processors and requires a written agreement.
results.modality_check: Equal-or-greater force. 'Shall conclude' and 'shall at minimum provide' are mandatory and not merely permissive.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. Requiring a written sub-processor agreement is equivalent to imposing the obligations by contract or other legal act, and the contract is not looser on timing, scope, or target.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in responding to data subject rights requests. The contract requires the processor, upon receiving such a request, to immediately forward it to the controller and not respond directly. That is a processor assistance obligation directed to the same underlying task.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are triggered by a data subject request to exercise data subject rights.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Must' is mandatory and at least as strong as 'shall assist'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. Immediate forwarding is consistent with assistance in responding to the request; the added prohibition on direct response is stricter, not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to ensure persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process them only on the controller's instructions. The contract instead imposes confidentiality on employees; it does not address instruction-based processing or limit processing to controller instructions.
results.condition_check: Compatible in the sense that both concern persons under the processor's authority with access to personal data, but the contract lacks the regulatory instruction condition entirely.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language, but it is about confidentiality rather than the required processing restriction.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's target is confidentiality/strict confidentiality, not the manner of processing on instructions from the controller.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s employees shall be subject to an obligation of confidentiality that ensures that the employees shall treat all the Personal Data under this Data Processor Agreement with strict confidentiality.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specified content (nature, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures), while the contract only requires providing a copy of the breach register on written request; that is a different information-access duty and does not state the notification contents.
results.condition_check: The regulation applies when notifying a personal data breach; the contract applies only if the controller or relevant data protection agency requests a copy in writing. These conditions are not the same.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory content requirement for notifications; the contract imposes a conditional obligation to provide a copy upon request. Even if mandatory when triggered, it is not the same obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation specifies particular items that must be included in the notification; the contract concerns a register copy, not those notification contents.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specified content (nature of breach, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures). The contract instead requires providing a register of personal data breaches in copy on request. That is an audit/record-disclosure duty, not the required notification content.
results.condition_check: The contract is triggered only if the Data Controller or relevant Data Protection Agency requests it in writing, whereas the regulation requires the notification content whenever a personal data breach notification is made.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a conditional obligation to provide a copy on request, but it does not impose the obligation to include the specified notification elements.
results.other_constraints_check: The object and locus differ: a register copy versus the contents of a breach notification. This is a different duty and does not supply the required items.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors under a general written authorization, while the contract instead requires written sub-processor agreements, monitoring/control of sub-processors, and providing documentation on request; it does not require advance notice of intended sub-processor changes.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered in case of general written authorization; the contract has no corresponding notice-on-change condition.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory obligations, but they are directed to different duties.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent timing or content obligation to inform of intended additions or replacements is present.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both concern breach-notification content, specifically the measures taken or proposed to mitigate/address the effects of a personal data breach; the contract text is an in-line item under a mandatory register clause and states that measure content directly.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply in the context of a personal data breach notification/register entry, with no materially different trigger.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('shall') and the sub-item is part of that mandatory minimum content.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract includes the mitigation-measures item, which is at least as specific as the regulation's 'measures taken or proposed to mitigate its effects'; no looser timing/location/target constraint appears.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for sub-processor performance. The contract requires written sub-processor agreements with equivalent data protection obligations and ongoing monitoring/control of sub-processors, but it does not state that the processor remains fully liable to the controller for sub-processor obligations.
results.condition_check: No special triggering condition in the regulation beyond sub-processor obligations; the contract addresses related subcontracting arrangements but not liability.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a liability obligation. The contract imposes contracting and monitoring duties, not liability.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location issue cures the missing liability commitment.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to consider risk when determining security level for personal data; the contract requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32, which covers the same security obligation and is broader/stricter.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are triggered in the context of security for personal data processing; the contract does not narrow the situation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses a mandatory 'shall' obligation, equal or stronger than the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract is at least as strict by requiring implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures under GDPR Article 32; it does not weaken the security standard or omit the relevant risk factors.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Compliance Monitoring Audit And Demonstration
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both require the processor to provide information/documentation to the controller to demonstrate compliance with GDPR/processor obligations.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds a written request condition, which is a reasonable trigger for making the documentation available.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall ... provide' is mandatory and at least as strong as 'shall make available'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'Documentation substantiating that' is substantively equivalent to information necessary to demonstrate compliance; the request timing/format is not looser.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall after the Data Controller’s written request hereof provide documentation substantiating that:

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires processor assistance specifically for notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority, while the contract clause concerns assisting the controller in responding to data subject rights requests by providing information and documentation.
results.condition_check: Different triggering situation: data subject rights requests versus personal data breach notification.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory 'shall assist', but it is directed to a different task.
results.other_constraints_check: The recipient/target and subject matter differ; there is no breach-notification or supervisory-authority notification obligation here.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject. The contract requires the processor to assist the controller, but only when the controller receives a data subject rights request and needs help providing information/documentation for the reply. That is a different assistance duty, not breach communication to the data subject.
results.condition_check: The triggering condition differs: breach communication under Art. 34 versus data subject rights requests and replies.
results.modality_check: Both use mandatory 'shall' language, so modality is not the issue.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's target/content is assistance by providing information and documentation for a rights request, not assistance in communicating a personal data breach; the subject matter and recipient are different.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the DPA to state the duration of the processing, and the contract states the DPA remains in force until the Main Agreement is terminated, which gives the agreement's duration.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the duration is tied to termination of the Main Agreement, which is a concrete end condition for the agreement's force.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall remain in force' is mandatory and at least as strong as the requirement to contain the duration.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the timing/duration is specified by an end point ('until the Main Agreement is terminated'), which satisfies the duration requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data processor Agreement shall remain in force until the Main Agreement is terminated. 10. Termination

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to implement organizational and technical measures to ensure security, including the Art. 32 measures; the contract obligates the Data Processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32, which covers the same security obligation and can be stricter by incorporating the law and agreement.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are general security-measure obligations without narrower triggering conditions.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses 'shall', matching the regulatory mandatory requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract’s reference to measures 'as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32' is at least as broad as the regulation’s security-measure requirement and does not weaken timing, manner, or scope.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject, but the contract only requires the processor to give immediate notice to the controller about a personal data breach; it does not require assistance with communicating to the data subject.
results.condition_check: Compatible in part: both are triggered by a personal data breach, though the contract defines it as a data security breach leading to accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to personal data.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall' obligation, which is not weaker than the regulation's obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds an immediate notice timing and a notice recipient of the Data Controller, but the required target of communication is the data subject; this is a different recipient and different action.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Same general security subject, but the contract only requires providing documentation of security measures on request; it does not require implementing appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure an appropriate level of security.
results.condition_check: Regulation is unconditional ongoing security implementation; contract is conditional on a written request for documentation.
results.modality_check: Contract uses a shall obligation, but only for documentation provision, not for the security measures themselves.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires substantive security measures proportionate to risk; the contract addresses disclosure of documentation about measures, which is a different and narrower duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation imposes controller liability for damage caused by infringing processing; the contract instead makes the processor accountable to the controller for sub-processors as for its own acts/omissions. Different actor and different obligation.
results.condition_check: Regulation is triggered by damage caused by processing that infringes the GDPR; the contract is a general accountability rule for sub-processors, not tied to that condition.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language, but it is not the same duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Different object and recipient: liability for damage versus accountability for sub-processor conduct.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: International Transfer Restrictions
results.core_alignment_check: processor/data transfer to third countries or international organizations: same substantive prohibition on transferring personal data abroad or to IOs
results.condition_check: Regulation allows transfer only with prior specific or general controller authorization; contract states no transfer at all, which is stricter and therefore compatible
results.modality_check: Regulation prohibits transfer absent authorization; contract prohibition is equal-or-greater force
results.other_constraints_check: Location/target constraint is stricter (only processed at processor's address) and does not weaken the transfer restriction
results.contract_source_text: The Personal Data is only processed by the Data Processor at the Data Processor’s address. The Data Processor does not transfer the Personal Data to third countries or international organizations.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures under Art. 32, and the contract obligates the Data Processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are framed generally as security measures for processing; the contract does not narrow the triggering situation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall implement' is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract references measures 'as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32,' which is coextensive with the regulation's security-measures obligation and not looser.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both concern the processor ensuring that processing of personal data occurs only on the controller's instructions; the contract expressly deems post-termination processing to be in accordance with the Instruction.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation applies to any natural person acting under the processor's authority with access to personal data; the contract addresses the processor's processing of the controller's personal data during a defined post-termination period, which is a narrower/specific situation and not contradictory.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the regulation imposes a mandatory restriction; the contract states the processing shall be considered in accordance with the Instruction, which is at least as strict.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the regulation's constraint is 'only on instructions from the controller'; the contract ties the processing to the Instruction and does not loosen that requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure that any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes them only on controller instructions. The contract says the processor's processing of the controller's personal data during the three months after termination is deemed to be in accordance with the Instruction, but it does not address natural persons acting under the processor's authority or impose the same instruction-only restriction on them.
results.condition_check: The regulation applies whenever a natural person acting under the processor's authority has access to personal data. The contract instead limits the clause to a post-termination three-month period and processing necessary/required under applicable law, which is a different and narrower situation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory/deeming language for its own processing, but it does not impose the required obligation on the relevant persons; it is not an equivalent obligation for the same actor and object.
results.other_constraints_check: The required constraint is 'only on instructions from the controller' for persons acting under the processor's authority. The contract's timing and lawful-processing qualifiers are different and do not supply that same target/constraint.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specified content (nature of breach, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures), while the contract only requires providing a register of personal data breaches on request; that is a different information duty and does not state the notification contents.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered by a personal data breach notification; the contract is triggered only if the controller or data protection agency requests a copy in writing.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory inclusion requirement; the contract imposes a conditional disclosure obligation, but for a different object and purpose.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation concerns the content of a notification; the contract concerns supplying a register copy, not the notification content or timing of breach notice.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes to the addition or replacement of sub-processors under a general written authorization. The contract instead requires written sub-processor agreements, equivalent data protection obligations, ongoing monitoring/control of sub-processors, and providing documentation of that monitoring on request. It does not require advance notice to the controller of intended sub-processor additions or replacements.
results.condition_check: No matching condition: the regulation is triggered by intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorization; the contract only addresses monitoring and documentation on request.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language for different duties, but it does not impose the required notification duty at all.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent timing, target, or content of notice is present; the only controller-facing element is documentation of monitoring if requested in writing, which is a different obligation.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both address processor-side handling of personal data under controller instructions, but the regulation specifically requires the processor to ensure persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process it only on controller instructions; the contract instead imposes confidentiality and a general no-processing-in-conflict-with-instructions rule on the processor itself, not the processor's personnel/authorized persons.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are tied to controller instructions / conflict with the Instruction, with the contract adding a written-consent exception from the controller.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses mandatory 'shall' and prohibitive 'may not', which is at least as strong as the regulation's obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Not fully aligned: the regulation targets natural persons acting under the processor's authority who have access to personal data; the contract does not expressly impose that personnel-level instruction-only constraint, though it is directionally related.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to ensure persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process them only on the controller's instructions; the contract sub-item merely says the processor complies with its obligations under the agreement and the Instruction, which is a general compliance statement and does not specifically impose or describe the required restriction on authorized persons' processing.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition is addressed in the contract clause beyond general compliance; it does not match the regulation's condition about persons acting under the processor's authority having access to personal data.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory compliance statement, but it is not the same obligation and does not expressly require the relevant persons to act only on controller instructions.
results.other_constraints_check: The required target and manner constraints (natural persons under the processor's authority, access to personal data, only on controller instructions) are absent; the clause is too general and does not cover these substantive constraints.
results.contract_source_text: the Data Processor complies with its obligations under this Data Processor Agreement and the Instruction; and

shared_groups: International Transfer Restrictions
results.core_alignment_check: Both concern processor restrictions on transferring personal data, but the regulation specifically targets transfers to a third country or international organization; the contract only prohibits transfers in conflict with instructions unless the controller agrees in writing, without mentioning third-country/international-organization transfers.
results.condition_check: Regulation requires no transfer without prior specific or general authorization of the controller; contract allows transfer if the controller has agreed in writing, which is compatible and at least as strict on authorization.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a prohibition ('may not') with an exception for controller written agreement, which is equal-or-greater force than the regulation's 'shall not without authorization'.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation's location/target constraint is specifically 'to a third country or international organization'; the contract's constraint is broader and different ('in conflict with the Instruction') and does not capture the required destination-based restriction.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both concern stating/containing the types of personal data processed under the DPA; the contract introduces an in-line list of the types of Personal Data processed in connection with the services.
results.condition_check: No special condition in the regulation; the contract ties it to processing in connection with delivery of the Main Services, which is the relevant processing context and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Mandatory/operative clause introducing the types processed; not merely permissive.
results.other_constraints_check: Equivalent substance and locus: the contract clause itself states the types of personal data, satisfying the content requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor processes the following types of Personal Data in connection with its delivery of the Main Services:

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes to add or replace sub-processors under a general written authorization, while the contract clause only states that listed sub-processors are pre-approved at the time of entering the agreement and does not impose any notice obligation about future changes.
results.condition_check: No matching condition: the regulation is triggered by intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors; the contract clause addresses initial approval at contract entry.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a declarative approval statement, not an obligation to inform or notify.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing and subject matter differ materially: initial approval versus advance notice of intended changes.
results.contract_source_text: The following Sub-Processors shall be considered approved by the data Controller at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement:

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to implement organizational and technical security measures to ensure processing security; the contract obligates the Data Processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32, which directly covers the same security-measures obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation frames measures as those that can include specified examples; the contract requires appropriate measures generally and ties them to Article 32, which is at least as broad and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall implement' is mandatory and meets the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract’s reference to measures 'as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32' does not loosen the timing, target, or manner; it incorporates the same security-measure framework and is not contradictory.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: The contract text is only a heading/topic label ('Personal Data Breaches') and does not state any obligation to notify or include the required breach-notification contents.
results.condition_check: No condition is stated.
results.modality_check: No modality/obligation is stated.
results.other_constraints_check: No manner, timing, target, location, or cause constraints are stated.
results.contract_source_text: Personal Data Breaches

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Both concern changes to sub-processors, but the contract only requires adding a new sub-processor to an internal list in sub-appendix B; it does not require informing the controller of intended additions or replacements.
results.condition_check: Regulation applies in case of general written authorization and intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors; the contract triggers on initiating sub-processing with a new sub-processor, which is narrower and omits replacement changes and the notice aspect.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a duty to inform the controller; the contract imposes a duty to update a list, not a notification duty.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's timing/location constraint is internal list update in sub-appendix B, not communication to the controller before or upon intended change.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement using the Sub- Processors listed in sub-appendix B. If the Data Processor initiates sub-processing with a new Sub-Processor, such new Sub-Processor shall be added to the list in sub-appendix B under paragraph 2. 7. Remuneration and costs

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to ensure persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; the contract only says personal data is processed by the processor at its address and not transferred abroad, which does not address instruction-based processing by authorized persons.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition in the contract matches the regulatory condition about persons acting under the processor's authority with access to personal data.
results.modality_check: The contract uses restrictive statements about where processing occurs and no transfer, but does not impose the required instruction-only obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Location and transfer constraints are unrelated to the required manner of processing on controller instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Personal Data is only processed by the Data Processor at the Data Processor’s address. The Data Processor does not transfer the Personal Data to third countries or international organizations.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation imposes a processor duty to ensure persons acting under the processor's authority process personal data only on the controller's instructions. The contract instead places a duty on the Data Controller about its own processing being lawful; it does not address the processor's personnel or instruction-only processing.
results.condition_check: The regulation concerns any natural person acting under the processor's authority with access to personal data; the contract concerns personal data transferred to the processor and processed by the controller. These are different situations.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a guarantee by the controller, but it is not an obligation on the processor to ensure instruction-only processing by persons under its authority.
results.other_constraints_check: The target and actor are different: the regulation is about persons acting under the processor's authority and access to personal data; the contract is about the controller's processing in accordance with law. No equivalent constraint is present.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Controller guarantees that the Personal Data transferred to the Data Processor is processed by the Data Controller in accordance with the Applicable Law, including the legislative requirements re lawfulness of processing.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to ensure that persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process them only on the controller's instructions. The contract says personal data may not be processed in conflict with the Instruction unless the controller has agreed in writing, which is the same instruction-only constraint on processing.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on controller instructions; the contract is at least as strict by prohibiting processing conflicting with the Instruction unless the controller agrees in writing.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation's 'shall ensure' is met by the contract's prohibitive 'may not' framing, which is equal-or-greater force.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The scope covers processing of personal data by those under the processor's authority; the contract's restriction on processing personal data in conflict with the Instruction is substantively equivalent and not looser.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract clause is a general compliance-with-law statement and does not specifically require processing personal data only on documented instructions from the controller.
results.condition_check: No relevant matching condition is stated.
results.modality_check: The clause is mandatory, but it does not impose the required instruction-based processing duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing, manner, target, or location constraints for documented instructions are provided.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor Agreement shall ensure that the Data Processor complies with the applicable data protection and privacy legislation (the ”Applicable Law”), including in particular:

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both address general written authorization for sub-processors: the processor must inform the controller of intended sub-processor changes/identity, and the controller has a right to object before the change takes effect.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation requires objection after being informed of intended changes; the contract requires written notice of the potential sub-processor before agreements/processsing and allows objection within seven days, which is at least as protective.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation gives the controller a right to object; the contract likewise gives the controller the right to object, framed as a notice obligation within a deadline.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract is stricter on timing and notice form (written notice, before agreements and before processing, within seven days) and concerns the same subject matter: addition/replacement of sub-processors under general authorization.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is given general authorization to engage third-parties to process the Personal Data (“Sub-Processors”) without obtaining any further written, specific authorization from the Data Controller, provided that the Data Processor notifies the Data Controller in writing about the identity of a potential Sub-Processor (and its processors, if any) before any agreements are made with the relevant Sub-Processors and before the relevant Sub-Processor processes any of the Personal Data. If the Data Controller wish to object to the relevant Sub- Processor, the Data Controller shall give notice hereof in writing within seven

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Partial overlap: the contract sub-item covers only one required notification element—the nature of the personal data breach—and adds affected data subject/registration counts, but it omits the required DPO contact details, consequences, and mitigation measures.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern the content of a personal data breach notification/register entry, with no conflicting trigger conditions.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('shall') and the sub-item is mandatory content within that register.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible on timing/location insofar as the contract is a register entry; however, it does not supply all required notification contents.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

results.core_alignment_check: processor/action/object align: contract prohibits processing personal data in conflict with the controller's instruction, which presupposes processing only per instruction; same real-world obligation as processing on documented instructions
results.condition_check: compatible: both are conditioned on controller instructions; contract adds written controller agreement for exceptions, which is stricter
