shared_groups: Controller Decision Rights And Remedial Powers
Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires that, under general written authorization, the controller be informed in advance of intended sub-processor additions/replacements and have the right to object. The parent clause gives the processor general authorization, requires prior written notice of the identity of a potential sub-processor before agreements/processsing, and expressly grants the controller a right to object in writing within seven calendar days of receiving the notification. The sub-item about absence of objections being deemed consent is consistent with that framework and does not remove the objection right.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both apply in the setting of general written authorization and prior notification of intended sub-processor changes.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract gives the controller an express right to object within a defined period; this is at least as strong as the regulation's right to object.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract requires notice before agreements are made and before the sub-processor processes personal data, which is equivalent to or stricter than being informed of intended changes beforehand; the seven-calendar-day response window is a permissible implementation detail.
results.contract_source_text: calendar days from receiving the notification from the Data Processor. Absence of any objections from the Data Controller shall be deemed a consent to the relevant Sub-Processor.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract imposes a direct security-measures obligation on the processor, but it does not undertake to assist the controller in ensuring security of processing.
results.condition_check: Compatible in subject matter, but the triggering situation is different: the regulation is an assistance duty to the controller, while the contract is a standalone implementation duty.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', which is mandatory, but it is mandatory for a different obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract references technical and organizational measures and Article 32, but it does not include the assistance-to-controller aspect required by the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the measures taken or proposed to mitigate the breach's effects, and the contract sub-item states a description of the measures the processor has taken or proposes to take to address the breach, including measures to mitigate adverse effects. This is the same content obligation, with the parent clause making it mandatory as part of the breach register.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern the personal data breach context; the contract item is triggered as part of the breach register for all personal data breaches, which is not weaker than the notification content requirement.
results.modality_check: Compliant: although the sub-item is descriptive, it sits under a mandatory parent clause ('shall have and maintain... shall at a minimum include'), so it carries mandatory force.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's wording on measures taken or proposed to address the breach and mitigate adverse effects matches the regulation's measures taken or proposed to mitigate effects; no looser timing, scope, or target constraints appear.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specified information (nature, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures). The contract only requires the processor to maintain a register of all personal data breaches and says the register shall include unspecified items, but it does not state the required notification contents here.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition is stated in either text beyond the existence of a personal data breach; the contract does not add a conflicting condition.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory 'shall' language, but it is about maintaining a register rather than making the breach notification content itself.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation concerns the contents of a notification; the contract concerns recordkeeping in a register and does not supply the required items or equivalent notification content.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of all Personal Data Breaches. The register shall at a minimum include the following:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority, while the contract sub-item only requires a description of measures taken or proposed to address/mitigate the breach; that is record content, not assistance with notification.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern a personal data breach context, with no materially different triggering condition.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory, but the sub-item itself only states content of the register; it does not impose the notification-assistance duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Not applicable to the required assistance/notification action; the clause addresses mitigation measures and register content instead of notifying the supervisory authority.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specified content (nature, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures). The contract only requires providing a register of personal data breaches in copy on request; that is an access/record-sharing duty, not the required notification content.
results.condition_check: The contract is triggered only if requested in writing by the Data Controller or relevant Data Protection Agency, whereas the regulation requires the contents of a breach notification whenever notification is made.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a conditional obligation to provide a copy on request, but it does not impose the affirmative duty to include the specified notification elements.
results.other_constraints_check: Different object and locus: a register/copy of records versus the notification itself and its mandatory contents. No equivalent inclusion of the listed items.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Compliance Monitoring Audit And Demonstration
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both are processor-to-controller information/audit-style obligations aimed at demonstrating GDPR/contract compliance; the contract requires documentation of monitoring and control to be provided to the controller on written request, which substantively overlaps with making available information necessary to demonstrate compliance.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by the controller's need/request for compliance-demonstrating information; the contract's 'if so requested in writing' is a reasonable request condition and not weaker in substance.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall be provided' is mandatory and at least as strong as 'shall make available'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is narrower in form (documentation of monitoring/control) but still provides compliance-demonstrating information to the controller; the request-in-writing timing/location constraint does not make it looser.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Compliance Monitoring Audit And Demonstration
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations. The contract instead requires return of personal data at termination and deletion with documentation of deletion. That is a different duty and does not provide the compliance information required.
results.condition_check: No material condition overlap beyond termination/possession status, which is unrelated to the regulation's compliance-information duty.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language, but for a different obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/location constraints are about termination and deletion documentation, not making compliance information available to the controller.
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the parent clause introduces an in-line list of the types of Personal Data processed, and the sub-item names a concrete data type ('Ordinary contact information on relevant employees from the Data Controller'), which is the same axis as the regulation's required types of personal data.
results.condition_check: No special condition in either text; both are unconditional content statements.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('processes the following types'), so the sub-item functions as part of the required mandatory specification.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the clause states a type/category of personal data in-line, not by cross-reference or placeholder, and the concrete instance named is a valid personal-data type.
results.contract_source_text: Ordinary contact information on relevant employees from the Data Controller.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires stating the types/categories of personal data processed, while the contract text only names a location/topic label ('Location of the Personal Data') and does not state any personal data types.
results.condition_check: No condition in either text.
results.modality_check: The regulation is mandatory, but the contract text is only a heading/label and contains no operative obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, or other substantive constraints are provided.
results.contract_source_text: Location of the Personal Data

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract clause is a general accountability/liability statement about the processor being responsible for sub-processors' actions and omissions, not an obligation to inform the controller of intended additions or replacements of sub-processors.
results.condition_check: No matching condition; the regulation is triggered in case of general written authorization and intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors, which the contract does not address.
results.modality_check: No comparable notification obligation is present; the clause is a statement of accountability, not a duty to inform.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, or location requirement for notice is addressed.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires processor assistance to the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject; the contract sub-item, read with the parent register clause, requires a description of measures taken or proposed to address the personal data breach, including mitigation measures. This is breach-response content, but it does not itself require assisting the controller in communicating to the data subject.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern a personal data breach context, but the contract is about measures to address/mitigate the breach rather than the communication task.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory, and the sub-item is mandatory as part of the required register contents.
results.other_constraints_check: No mismatch on timing/location, but the substantive object differs: internal register content versus assistance in communicating the breach to the data subject.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: processor processing personal data under the controller's instruction/documented instruction requirement; the clause deems post-termination processing to be in accordance with the Instruction, which addresses the same processor-on-instructions obligation
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation requires documented instructions from the controller; the clause ties processing to the Instruction and limits it to a defined post-termination period and legal necessity, which is not weaker on the instruction condition
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract imposes a mandatory processing rule and deems such processing to be in accordance with the Instruction; this is at least as strong as the regulatory shall
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the timing/limit (up to three months after termination, only as necessary and required by applicable law) is an added restriction, not a loosening, and does not contradict the instruction requirement
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the controller's identity and contact details to be contained in the DPA, while the contract clause only points to contact information for both the Data Processor and Data Controller being provided in the Main Agreement; it does not itself state the controller's identity/contact details in the DPA.
results.condition_check: No condition in the regulation or contract.
results.modality_check: The regulation is mandatory, but the contract clause is a descriptive pointer rather than a mandatory inclusion of the required details.
results.other_constraints_check: The required location/content is the DPA itself; the contract instead defers to the Main Agreement, which is a different document and a pointer rather than the content.
results.contract_source_text: The contact information for the Data Processor and the Data Controller is provided in the Main Agreement..

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation allows the processor to process personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law. The contract instead restricts processing to documented instructions from the controller and a stated service purpose, with no carve-out for processing required by law without instructions.
results.condition_check: The regulation's condition is a legal requirement under Union or Member State law; the contract's condition is compliance with documented controller instructions and service purpose. These are not the same and the contract omits the legal-law exception.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a restrictive 'may only' formulation, which is strong, but it is stronger in the wrong direction because it does not permit the legally required exception.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is narrower on target and scope of permitted processing, limiting it to documented instructions and Main Services, whereas the regulation requires allowance for law-required processing even absent documented instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller; the contract sub-item merely says the processor complies with its obligations under the agreement and the Instruction, which is a general compliance statement and does not itself state the processing-on-instructions rule.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition in the regulation beyond documented instructions; the contract does not address that condition as such.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory compliance statement, but it is not the same obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/manner/location constraints are stated for the required processing-on-instructions duty.
results.contract_source_text: the Data Processor complies with its obligations under this Data Processor Agreement and the Instruction; and

shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment
results.core_alignment_check: The contract text is only a heading/topic label for DPIAs and prior consultation; it does not itself state the required DPIA contents (systematic description, necessity/proportionality assessment, risk assessment, or measures).
results.condition_check: No operative condition is stated.
results.modality_check: No obligation modality is stated; this is just a section title.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, or other substantive constraints are provided.
results.contract_source_text: Data protection impact assessments and prior consultation

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to contractually impose the Article 28(3) GDPR obligations on engaged sub-processors. The contract clause instead gives general authorization to engage sub-processors and requires prior written notice/objection handling; it does not state that the same Article 28(3) obligations are passed down to sub-processors.
results.condition_check: The regulation applies when the processor engages sub-processors. The contract also concerns engagement of sub-processors, so the subject matter is related, but it is about authorization and notice rather than downstream obligation imposition.
results.modality_check: The regulation uses a mandatory 'shall impose' duty. The contract does not contain an equivalent obligation to impose those obligations on sub-processors; it only permits engagement subject to notice and objection.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires doing so 'by way of contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law.' The contract clause instead sets a notification/objection procedure and timing before agreements are made and before processing starts, which is a different constraint and does not supply the required downstream contractual imposition.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is given general authorization to engage third-parties to process the Personal Data (“Sub-Processors”) without obtaining any further written, specific authorization from the Data Controller, provided that the Data Processor notifies the Data Controller in writing about the identity of a potential Sub-Processor (and its processors, if any) before any agreements are made with the relevant Sub-Processors and before the relevant Sub-Processor processes any of the Personal Data. If the Data Controller wish to object to the relevant Sub- Processor, the Data Controller shall give notice hereof in writing within seven

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation imposes controller liability for damage caused by infringing processing under GDPR Article 82(2). The contract instead requires the controller to indemnify the processor for documented additional costs caused by changes in applicable law or guidance/court practice. Different actor, different trigger, different object, and different recipient.
results.condition_check: Not compatible: the regulation is triggered by damage caused by GDPR-infringing processing; the contract is triggered by changes in law/guidance/court practice causing extra costs.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory indemnity ('shall indemnify'), but it is a different obligation from liability for GDPR damage.
results.other_constraints_check: Different subject matter and scope: indemnification of costs versus liability for damage; no overlap in timing, target, or causal conditions relevant to Article 82(2).
results.contract_source_text: If changes to the Applicable Law, including new guidance or courts practice, result in additional costs to the Data Processor, the Data Controller shall indemnify the Data Processor of such documented costs. 8. Breach and liability

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation permits processing without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law; the contract instead restricts the processor to act/process only in accordance with documented instructions from the controller and only for delivering the main services. This does not include the legal-law exception.
results.condition_check: The regulation's condition is a requirement under Union or Member State law; the contract's condition is processing only under documented controller instruction and service purpose. These are not the same and the contract is narrower.
results.modality_check: The regulation grants a permission/exception; the contract imposes a restrictive obligation ('may only'), which is not contradictory in force but does not cover the legal exception.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds a purpose limitation and documented-instruction constraint, but omits the specific allowance for processing without documented instructions when required by law.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to ensure persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process them only on the controller's instructions. The contract sub-item instead says the processor complies with applicable law in respect of processing the controller's personal data, which is a general legal-compliance statement and does not specifically impose the instruction-only restriction on authorized persons.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition in the contract matches the regulation's condition about persons acting under the processor's authority having access to personal data.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a general compliance obligation, not an obligation to ensure the specific instruction-only processing rule.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation's target is a specific manner of processing by authorized natural persons; the contract does not address that target, timing, or locus, and the parent clause only concerns providing documentation substantiating compliance after request, which is a separate audit/documentation duty.
results.contract_source_text: the Data Processor complies with the Applicable Law in respect of the processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires prior specific or general written authorization from the controller before engaging a sub-processor, while the contract requires a written sub-processor agreement and ongoing monitoring/control of sub-processors; it does not require controller authorization.
results.condition_check: The regulation's trigger is engaging a sub-processor without prior written authorization; the contract's trigger is entering into sub-processor agreements and monitoring compliance, which is a different condition.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a prohibition absent authorization; the contract imposes affirmative duties to contract and monitor, but not the authorization condition.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/target constraint of prior written authorization by the controller is missing; the contract instead addresses post-engagement contractual controls and documentation on request.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for a sub-processor's obligations, while the contract clause only addresses remuneration for adapting to changed instructions and exemption from liability for non-performance of the main agreement; it does not allocate liability for sub-processor obligations.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition overlap; the contract's conditions concern changed instructions and impossibility of delivery, not sub-processor performance.
results.modality_check: The contract is permissive/exemptive on liability for non-performance in certain cases, but it does not impose the required liability for sub-processor obligations.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching target/object or scope: the regulation is about liability to the controller for sub-processor obligations, which is absent from the contract.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is also entitled to remuneration for any time and material used to adapt and change the processing activities in order to comply with any changes to the Data Controller’s Instruction, including implementation costs and additional costs required to deliver the Main Services due to the change in the Instruction. The Data Processor is exempted from liability for non-performance with the Main Agreement if the performance of the obligations under the Main Agreement would be in conflict with any changed Instruction or if contractual delivery in accordance with the changed Instruction is impossible. This could for instance be the case;

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires a controller right to object to intended additions or replacements of sub-processors after prior notice from the processor; the contract clause only states that sub-processors have been added and communicated prior to sub-processing, and does not grant any objection right or address changes/replacements.
results.condition_check: Compatible only in part: both concern notice about sub-processors, but the contract does not cover the triggering condition of intended changes or the controller's objection opportunity.
results.modality_check: Weaker/absent: the regulation requires a right for the controller; the contract merely states a communication fact and imposes no corresponding right or obligation to object.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing of prior communication is present, but the required target of the notice (intended changes concerning addition or replacement) and the post-notice objection mechanism are missing.
results.contract_source_text: The following Sub-Processors have been added and communicated to the Data Controller prior to the relevant sub-processing:

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation gives the controller a right to object to intended additions/replacements of sub-processors after notice from the processor; the contract instead states that listed sub-processors are pre-approved at signing, which does not provide the controller's objection right or the same change-notification mechanism.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation is triggered by intended changes to sub-processors after the controller is informed; the contract concerns approval of existing listed sub-processors at the time of agreement.
results.modality_check: Weaker/absent: the regulation requires a controller right to object; the contract only states approval and does not grant an objection right.
results.other_constraints_check: Different target and timing: the regulation concerns additions or replacements of sub-processors and prior information by the processor; the contract only addresses approval at contract entry for following sub-processors.
results.contract_source_text: The following Sub-Processors shall be considered approved by the data Controller at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement:

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to ensure that any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes them only on the controller's instructions. The contract instead defines personal data and lists categories/types of data in an appendix, plus limits processing to what is necessary for the main services. It does not impose the instruction-only rule on persons acting under the processor's authority.
results.condition_check: The regulation's condition is access to personal data by natural persons acting under the processor's authority; the contract has no corresponding condition or instruction-based restriction.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory safeguard obligation; the contract's relevant text is about data classification and service-necessity limits, not a mandatory instruction-compliance duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent timing, manner, target, or location constraint is provided for the required instruction-only processing rule.
results.contract_source_text: (the ”Personal Data”). The categories and types of Personal Data processed by the Data Processor on behalf of the Data Controller are listed in sub-appendix A. The Data Processor only performs processing activities that are necessary and relevant to perform the Main Services. The parties shall update sub-appendix A whenever changes occur that necessitates an update.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to ensure that any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes them only on the controller's instructions. The contract instead addresses what categories/types of personal data are processed, that processing is limited to necessary/relevant main services, and that an appendix is updated when changes occur. It does not impose the specific instruction-only rule for persons acting under the processor's authority.
results.condition_check: The regulation's condition is access to personal data by natural persons acting under the processor's authority; the contract does not address that situation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language for its own listed obligations, but not for the required instruction-only restriction.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation's target is the conduct of natural persons under the processor's authority; the contract's target is the scope/listing of processing activities and data categories, which is a different duty.
results.contract_source_text: (the ”Personal Data”). The categories and types of Personal Data processed by the Data Processor on behalf of the Data Controller are listed in sub-appendix A. The Data Processor only performs processing activities that are necessary and relevant to perform the Main Services. The parties shall update sub-appendix A whenever changes occur that necessitates an update.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the DPA to state the duration of processing, and the contract specifies a concrete processing duration/retention period of up to three months after termination.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract ties the post-termination processing period to necessity and applicable law, which is a narrower/conditional duration but still states the duration required.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses mandatory language for the processing period and thus meets the requirement to contain the duration.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the timing/location constraint is specific and directly addresses the processing period after termination; it is not contradictory or looser than the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the DPA to state the duration of processing. The contract states that the processor will continue processing personal data for up to three months after termination, which directly specifies a processing duration.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract gives a concrete duration and a lawful-necessity qualifier, which is not weaker than the regulation's bare duration requirement.
results.modality_check: Mandatory/operative wording is present in the parent clause and sub-item; the duration is stated as part of the processor's obligation, not merely permitted.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing constraint is equivalent and specific: 'up to three months after termination' clearly contains the processing duration required by the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment
Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract text is only a topic heading ('Data protection impact assessments and prior consultation') and does not itself impose processor assistance or any substantive obligation.
results.condition_check: No condition stated in the contract text.
results.modality_check: No operative modality stated in the contract text.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, or other constraints stated in the contract text.

shared_groups: Compliance Monitoring Audit And Demonstration
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations to the controller. The sub-item itself only states that the processor complies with applicable law regarding processing of the controller’s personal data; that is a general compliance statement, not the required provision of information/documentation. The parent clause adds that, after the controller’s written request, the processor shall provide documentation substantiating that statement, which is closer to an audit/documentation duty and can cover compliance evidence.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation has no special trigger beyond the processor’s duty, while the parent clause conditions provision on the controller’s written request, which is a reasonable and narrower trigger for making documentation available.
results.modality_check: The parent clause uses 'shall' and therefore imposes a mandatory obligation; the sub-item is descriptive content within that mandatory clause.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires making information available to the controller for demonstrating compliance. The parent clause requires providing documentation substantiating compliance, which is substantively aligned with an information/documentation availability duty, though it is narrower because it is only on written request and framed as documentation rather than all necessary information.
results.contract_source_text: the Data Processor complies with the Applicable Law in respect of the processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for sub-processor performance. The contract requires written sub-processor agreements with equivalent data protection obligations and ongoing monitoring/control of sub-processors, but it does not state that the processor remains fully liable to the controller for sub-processor obligations.
results.condition_check: No special triggering condition beyond sub-processor performance; the contract addresses related sub-processor management generally, but not the liability condition.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory liability obligation; the contract imposes mandatory agreement/monitoring duties, but not the liability obligation itself.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds monitoring, documentation, and written-agreement requirements, which are different and do not supply the missing liability-to-controller commitment.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority, while the contract clause only requires forwarding data subject rights requests to the controller and not responding directly.
results.condition_check: Different trigger: data subject rights request versus personal data breach notification.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Same obligation: processor processing personal data only in accordance with the controller's documented instruction. The clause says post-termination processing is considered in accordance with the Instruction, which addresses the same instruction-based processing requirement.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation requires documented instructions from the controller; the clause ties the processing to 'the Instruction' and does not introduce a conflicting condition.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses a mandatory deeming statement that the processing shall be considered in accordance with the Instruction, which is not weaker than the regulatory requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the clause is narrower in time (three months after termination) and adds an applicable-law necessity qualifier, but these do not make it looser with respect to the instruction requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure that natural persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process them only on the controller's instructions. The contract says the processor's processing of the controller's personal data during the three months after termination is deemed to be in accordance with the Instruction, which addresses processing on instructions, but it does not specifically impose the rule on natural persons acting under the processor's authority who have access to the data.
results.condition_check: The contract's condition is limited to the three months after termination and necessity under applicable law, whereas the regulation applies generally to any natural person acting under the processor's authority with access to personal data.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a deeming/interpretive statement about processing being in accordance with the Instruction, not an explicit obligation ensuring all relevant persons only process on controller instructions.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation targets persons acting under the processor's authority and their access to personal data; the contract instead addresses the processor's own processing during a post-termination period and backup inclusion, which is a different scope and timing.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation imposes processor liability for damage only when the processor breaches GDPR processor-specific obligations or acts outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract instead addresses remuneration for adapting processing to changed instructions and exempts the processor from liability for non-performance when performance conflicts with changed instructions or delivery is impossible. It does not state the processor's liability for damage under the GDPR standard, nor the specific liability trigger tied to unlawful instructions or processor-specific GDPR breaches.
results.condition_check: The contract refers to changed instructions and conflict/impossibility of performance, which is not the same as liability arising from failure to comply with GDPR processor obligations or acting outside/contrary to lawful instructions.
results.modality_check: The regulation is a liability rule ('shall be liable only where...'); the contract provides an exemption from liability in certain circumstances and a right to remuneration, which is not the same obligation and does not cover the required liability standard.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching substantive constraints on damage caused by processing, processor-specific GDPR obligations, or unlawfulness of instructions are set out; the clause is about contract performance under changed instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is also entitled to remuneration for any time and material used to adapt and change the processing activities in order to comply with any changes to the Data Controller’s Instruction, including implementation costs and additional costs required to deliver the Main Services due to the change in the Instruction. The Data Processor is exempted from liability for non-performance with the Main Agreement if the performance of the obligations under the Main Agreement would be in conflict with any changed Instruction or if contractual delivery in accordance with the changed Instruction is impossible. shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority; the contract instead requires forwarding data-subject rights requests to the controller and not responding directly. Different actor-task-object-recipient.

results.modality_check: Mandatory in both, but on different obligations.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing/location/cause constraints relevant to breach-notification assistance.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for a sub-processor's performance, while the contract instead limits each party's cumulative liability to a capped amount based on payments. This is a different and contradictory liability allocation, not the same obligation.
results.condition_check: No relevant matching condition; the regulation is unconditional and the contract introduces a general liability cap tied to breach circumstances and payment history.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes full liability; the contract imposes a limitation/cap, which is materially weaker and contrary to the requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's scope and amount constraints are looser and inconsistent with 'remain fully liable'; it does not preserve full liability for sub-processor obligations.
results.contract_source_text: Each party’s cumulated liability under this Data Processor Agreement is limited to the payments made under the Main Agreement in the 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract. If the Data Processor Agreement has not been in force for 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract, the limited liability amount shall be calculated proportionately based on the actual performed payments.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation permits processing without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law; the contract instead imposes confidentiality and a general prohibition on processing contrary to instructions, with controller-written consent as the exception.
results.condition_check: Different conditions: regulation applies when processing is required by Union or Member State law; contract applies unless the controller has written agreement and only addresses conflict with instructions.
results.modality_check: The contract is more restrictive, but it does not grant the same lawful-processing exception; it is not aligned with the legal-authority condition.
results.other_constraints_check: Different object and target: the regulation concerns processing personal data without documented instructions; the contract concerns confidentiality and processing only in accordance with instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

results.condition_check: Not applicable / no matching condition stated.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory, but the sub-item is only a citation to legislation, not an operative obligation matching the required duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive overlap on the required action/object/recipient; this is a legal-reference clause, not a processing-instructions clause.
results.contract_source_text: The European Parliament and the Council’s Directive 95/46/EF of 24 October 1995 on the protection of individuals with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data as implemented in Danish law with, among others, the Act on Processing of Personal Data (Act No. 429 of 31 May 2000).

This is related assistance, but it does not expressly provide broader help in fulfilling the controller's obligation to respond; it only forwards the request and avoids direct response.

results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds immediate forwarding and a prohibition on direct response, which are compatible and not weaker than the regulation's assistance duty, but they do not cover the full assistance obligation.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes involving addition or replacement of sub-processors under a general written authorization. shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage

The contract instead imposes a general contractual cap on each party’s cumulative liability for breach of contract based on prior payments. That is a different obligation and does not address the processor’s GDPR damage-liability conditions.
results.condition_check: Not aligned. The regulation’s trigger conditions are processor non-compliance with GDPR processor obligations or acting outside/contrary to lawful instructions; the contract’s trigger is the occurrence of circumstances leading to a breach of contract, which is materially different.
results.modality_check: The regulation is a liability rule with a specific scope/exception structure; the contract is a limitation-of-liability clause. It does not impose the same liability basis or exception structure, though it is mandatory as a contract term.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the controller's right to object after being informed of intended additions/replacements of sub-processors; the contract instead requires the processor to conclude agreements with sub-processors, monitor/control compliance, and provide documentation on request. It does not give the controller notice of intended changes or a right to object.

results.modality_check: The regulation grants a controller right; the contract imposes processor duties, but not the required controller right or equivalent restriction on changes.

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

results.other_constraints_check: The regulation concerns making information available to the controller; the contract instead speaks of providing documentation substantiating compliance, which is a compatible and potentially stricter form of availability, but the specific sub-item does not itself identify the required information.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller before processing when not prohibited by public-interest law grounds; the contract instead says the data controller guarantees its own processing complies with lawfulness requirements. Different actor, different action, different object.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / different condition; the regulation has a specific pre-processing notice condition tied to legal prohibition on informing the controller, which is absent.
results.modality_check: The contract is a guarantee by the controller, not an obligation on the processor to inform; modality does not match the required duty.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing ('before processing') and the specific recipient (the controller) are not addressed; the clause concerns lawful processing generally, not notice of a legal requirement.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the DPA to contain the duration of processing, and the contract states that the processor may continue processing personal data for up to three months after termination, which specifies the processing duration.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds a condition that continued processing is only to the extent necessary and required under applicable law, which is not weaker than the regulation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses mandatory/authorizing language within a binding clause and specifies the duration; it is not merely permissive in a way that would undercut the requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The timing is concrete and within the required duration-content obligation; specifying 'up to three months after termination' directly states the duration of processing.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Partial overlap only: both concern security measures and documentation, but the regulation requires implementing organizational and technical measures to ensure security (including encryption, confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience, recovery, and regular testing/evaluation), while the contract only requires the processor to provide documentation of its security measures on written request.
results.condition_check: Compatible only as to a request trigger, but the regulation is not request-triggered; it is an ongoing substantive security obligation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory disclosure duty, but it is about providing documentation, not performing the security measures themselves.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is narrower in object and target: documentation of security measures to the controller on written request, not the actual security measures or their effectiveness.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

results.other_constraints_check: The timing ('immediately') and forwarding/refraining constraints are specific to request handling and do not match DPIA assistance.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the DPA to state the nature and purpose of the processing. results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract references GDPR Article 35 DPIAs and Article 36 prior consultation, which aligns with the regulatory context and does not impose a looser timing, scope, or location constraint.

results.modality_check: N/A - no matching obligation to assist in breach communication is present
results.other_constraints_check: N/A - timing and directness constraints relate to forwarding requests, not breach notification assistance
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

results.modality_check: The contract gives notice-and-objection mechanics, not a termination right.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent timing, manner, or target for termination is provided.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the DPA to state the types of personal data processed; the contract clause instead addresses post-termination processing, backup, and deemed instruction, not the categories/types of personal data.
results.condition_check: Not applicable; the clause has no matching content requirement about listing data types.
results.modality_check: Not applicable; the clause does not impose the required content obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Not applicable; timing/termination language concerns a different duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the DPA to contain the types of personal data (a content/disclosure obligation), while the contract clause only addresses post-termination processing, backup, and lawful instruction compliance. It does not state any types of personal data processed.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition overlap; the contract's three-month post-termination condition is unrelated to listing data types.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory/permitted language for processing and backup, but this does not bear on the required content obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching content, timing, or location constraint for specifying types of personal data is present.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to impose Article 28(3)-equivalent obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract or other legal act. The contract text only says the processor is using sub-processors listed in an appendix and that new sub-processors must be added to that list; it does not impose any GDPR-style obligations on those sub-processors.
results.condition_check: No relevant matching condition. The regulation concerns engaged sub-processors and contractual/legal imposition of obligations; the clause only concerns listing and adding sub-processors.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language for adding names to a list, but not for imposing the required obligations on sub-processors.
results.other_constraints_check: The only constraint is inclusion in sub-appendix B / adding new sub-processors there, which is a different administrative requirement and not the required contractual flow-down of Article 28(3) duties.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement using the Sub- Processors listed in sub-appendix B. If the Data Processor initiates sub-processing with a new Sub-Processor, such new Sub-Processor shall be added to the list in sub-appendix B under paragraph 2. 7. Remuneration and costs

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Processor may process personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law vs processor shall continue processing personal data for up to three months after termination to the extent necessary and required under applicable law; same actor/process, but the contract ties it to post-termination continuation and deems it in accordance with the instruction rather than expressly addressing absence of documented instructions.
results.condition_check: Compatible in part: both invoke a legal requirement under applicable law, but the contract adds a specific post-termination three-month period and necessity condition rather than the regulation's broader 'without documented instructions' situation.
results.modality_check: Contract is mandatory ('shall') and therefore not weaker than the regulation's permission/exception.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/location constraints are materially different: the contract limits the processing to up to three months after termination and includes backup processing, which is not the same as the regulation's general exception for processing without documented instructions when law requires it.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the parent clause is a processor duty to maintain a breach register, and the sub-item states content of that register (description of breach nature and affected data subjects/records), which is related to breach handling but not the same as assisting the controller in communicating the breach to the data subject.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation has no special triggering condition beyond a personal data breach; the clause applies to all breaches in the register.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('shall'), so the sub-item content is mandatory within that register.
results.other_constraints_check: Not aligned: the regulation requires assistance in communicating the breach to the data subject, while the clause only requires recording descriptive information about the breach; it does not impose communication/assistance to the controller or data subject.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

shared_groups: Controller Decision Rights And Remedial Powers
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires that, after being informed of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors, the controller has a right to object. The contract parent/sub-item instead concerns changes to the Data Controller’s Instruction and says changes are only applicable before implementation if the controller explicitly requires that. This is a different obligation about instruction changes, not sub-processor changes or a controller objection right.
results.condition_check: The triggering condition is not the same: the regulation is triggered by intended changes to sub-processors after notice; the contract is triggered by changes to the controller's instruction and their applicability before implementation.
results.modality_check: The regulation grants the controller a right to object; the contract gives the controller a right to require changes to be applicable before implementation, which is not the same right and does not impose an objection mechanism.
results.other_constraints_check: The subject matter and timing differ materially: sub-processor addition/replacement versus instruction changes, and notice of intended changes versus pre-implementation applicability.
results.contract_source_text: where the Data Controller explicitly requires that the changes to the Instruction shall be applicable before the changes can be implemented; and

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes to sub-processors (addition or replacement) under general written authorization. The contract instead says new sub-processors shall be added to a list in sub-appendix B; it addresses listing/recording sub-processors, not notifying the controller of intended changes.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered in case of general written authorization and intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors. The contract speaks generally about initiating sub-processing with a new sub-processor and updating a list, without the notification trigger or replacement-change aspect.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes an affirmative duty to inform. The contract imposes a duty to add the new sub-processor to a list, which is not the same obligation and does not provide the required notification.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's only relevant constraint is updating sub-appendix B; this is a different location/content-maintenance duty, not the required communication to the controller.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement using the Sub- Processors listed in sub-appendix B. shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority. The contract sub-item only states content for a breach register: a description of the nature of the breach and, if possible, affected data subject/registration counts. That is record content, not assistance with notification.

results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory ('shall have and maintain'), but the sub-item itself is only descriptive content within a register and does not impose the required assistance obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation's target is notification to the supervisory authority. The contract concerns maintaining a breach register and its contents, which is a different duty and location; it does not require notifying or assisting notification.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the DPA to state the types of personal data processed; the contract only gives a generic legal definition of 'personal data' rather than naming the actual types/categories of data covered by the agreement.

results.modality_check: The contract uses a definitional statement, not an obligation to contain the required types.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location constraints relevant.
results.contract_source_text: ”Personal data” include “any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person” as defined in GDPR, article 4

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires implementing/maintaining security measures for processing (including encryption, confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience, timely restoration, and regular testing/evaluation), while the contract only requires the processor to provide documentation of its security measures on written request.
results.condition_check: The contract is conditional on a written request by the controller; the regulation is an ongoing substantive security obligation, not a request-triggered disclosure duty.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', but it is an obligation to provide documentation, not to implement the security measures themselves.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract concerns documentation and written-request timing only; it does not address the required security content, manner, or operational measures.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation imposes GDPR damage liability on any controller involved in processing for damage caused by infringing processing. The contract clause instead caps each party’s contractual liability under the DPA to a payment-based amount, which is a different obligation and does not address GDPR tort/statutory liability for processing damage.
results.condition_check: Not applicable: the regulation concerns damage caused by processing that infringes the GDPR; the contract concerns circumstances leading to a breach of contract and payment history.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a limitation of liability, not an assumption of liability for GDPR processing damage.
results.other_constraints_check: Different subject matter and target: contractual liability cap versus statutory controller liability for GDPR infringement; no equivalent coverage of the required duty.
results.contract_source_text: Each party’s cumulated liability under this Data Processor Agreement is limited to the payments made under the Main Agreement in the 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract. If the Data Processor Agreement has not been in force for 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract, the limited liability amount shall be calculated proportionately based on the actual performed payments.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the DPA to state the duration of the processing; the contract clause instead limits processing to documented instructions and a service purpose, but does not specify any processing duration.
results.condition_check: Not applicable; the requirement is an inclusion/content requirement, and the clause does not provide the required duration.
results.modality_check: Not applicable; the clause’s restrictive 'may only' language is not the issue because the required content is absent.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing/duration term or equivalent processing period is stated; the clause only gives purpose and instruction constraints.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specific content items (nature of breach, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures), while the contract only requires immediate notice of a personal data breach and defines what counts as a breach; it does not state the required notification contents.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by a personal data breach.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract imposes a mandatory immediate notice obligation, which is not weaker than the regulation's notification duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible on timing and target: immediate notice to the Data Controller is at least as strict as the regulation's notification timing, but the required content is missing.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the controller’s right to object to intended additions/replacements of sub-processors after notice; the contract instead requires written sub-processor agreements, equivalent data protection obligations, ongoing monitoring/control, and documentation on request. It does not grant an objection right or notice of intended changes.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation is triggered by intended changes to the addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorization; the contract addresses sub-processor contracting and monitoring generally, not that notice-and-objection situation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory obligations, but they are obligations of a different kind and do not include the required right for the controller.

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

This is related compliance information, but it is narrower and focused only on sub-processor monitoring documentation, not all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with the processor's Article 28 obligations.
results.condition_check: Both are triggered by a request/requested disclosure to the controller; compatible.
